wide_field_scan_result.important_negative_result: The already-mapped state-0x50 packet builder does not directly read controller +0xC0. Its relevant inputs remain route selector +0x20, override/source mode +0x100, optional token +0x120, primary string +0x70, and identity-tail fields.

key_conversion: 0x140154F69..0x140154F73 calls 0x14002DBD0 to normalize/copy the decimal key string into [rsp+0x20]

map_write.key: RDX = decimal string derived from controller +0xC0
map_write.constructor_context: controller +0xAA8 is the settings map constructed with literal totp by 0x140151BE0

value_source.observation: The value written into the totp map is loaded into EBX from the four bytes immediately behind the packet/buffer cursor after the preceding byte-copy/decode sequence. This is best treated as a server- or stream-sourced dword until its upstream packet field is fully named.

consumer_path.entry_guard: 0x140156669 compares controller +0x10 against 0x0A before entering the lookup body
pre_lookup_cleanup: clears/release old pointer at [controller +0x30] +0x40 when present
clears/release old pointer at [controller +0x30] +0x2B8 when present
key_conversion: 0x14015670A..0x140156714 calls 0x14002DBD0 to normalize/copy the decimal key string into [rsp+0x30]

map_lookup.key: R8 = decimal string derived from controller +0xC0

lifecycle_connection.constructor_context: 0x140151BE0 constructs two adjacent settings maps: controller +0xAA8 with literal totp and controller +0xB70 with literal login. It then stores controller +0xC38 = atotp and controller +0xC40 = dn.
resolved_chain: request-prep derives controller +0xC0 from explicit selected values, optional numeric token +0x120, primary-string hash, or login-map fallback key atotp
producer path 0x140154F31 converts +0xC0 to a decimal string and writes a stream-derived dword into the totp map at controller +0xAA8
consumer path 0x140156676 converts the same +0xC0 to a decimal string and looks that value back up from the totp map
lookup output is stored into controller +0xBC and success is tracked in controller +0xB9 before state advances to 0x0D
lifecycle_connection.conclusion: controller +0xC0 is no longer just a generic request hash. In the login/TOTP slice it is the canonical key that bridges the login atotp fallback and the separate totp map cache.

opennxt_947_impact: A faithful 947 login/auth model should keep the login map key atotp and the totp map root separate, then correlate them through the decimal string form of controller +0xC0.
The value stored under the totp key appears as a dword recovered from the incoming or decoded stream path, then later copied into controller +0xBC with controller +0xB9 marking lookup success.
State-0x50 packet selection should not be treated as the direct +0xC0 consumer. The direct +0xC0 consumers are the totp-map producer and lookup paths around states 0x0A through 0x0D.
next_targets: Map the upstream packet/buffer field that becomes EBX before 0x140154F97 writes it into the totp map.
Name controller +0xBC and +0xB9 by following their later readers after state 0x0D.
Recover the state-machine predecessor that reaches the fallthrough block 0x140154F31, since direct-call and address-reference scans show it is not a normal direct-call target.
Correlate OpenNXT 947 login/TOTP response fields with the client dword stored in the +0xAA8 totp map.
